description:
Pokepelago: A collection-based world where you catch 'em all by guessing their names.
Each game region acts as a zone gated by a Region Pass item.
Type Keys gate access to Pokemon of that type (creates cross-player dependencies).

v0.2.0.description:
# Release v0.2.0

Welcome to [Poképelago](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/), a Pokémon collection and guessing game built for Archipelago Multiworld play!

## What's New

- **Sprites without downloading:** Just paste a sprite repo URL (like the [PokeAPI sprites](https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites/tree/master/sprites)) into Settings and sprites load automatically. No ZIP, no upload required
- **Better connection handling:** The game now reliably detects when the server goes offline and shows you the connection quality in real time
- **Name guessing improvements:** Pokémon with accents, dots, or localized names (Japanese, French, etc.) are now recognized correctly
- **Stability:** Various generation and logic fixes that prevented some seeds from generating. For stability reasons, only generation 1 through 3 are accessible for now.

---

## How to Play

### For Players

1. **Setup Your YAML:** Download the `pokepelago.yaml` from this release. Edit it to pick your generation and goal, then hand it to your host.
2. **Open the game:** Go to **[https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/)** - Please be aware - this is a new URL! The old URL will remain available to support old generations (for now).
3. **Load sprites (optional but recommended):** In Settings → _Sprite Repo URL_, paste this link and sprites will load live:
    https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites/tree/master/sprites

Or upload your own sprite folder the old way. No sprites? The game still plays fully. Please be aware that using sprites is at your own disclosure.

4. **Connect:** Hit the gear icon, enter your server address, slot name, and password, then click **Connect**. Start guessing Pokémon names — a correct guess means you caught it!

### For Hosts

1. Download the `pokepelago.apworld` from this release.
2. Place it in your Archipelago installation's `custom_worlds` folder.
3. Collect `pokepelago.yaml` files from your players and put them in your `Players` folder.
4. Run **Archipelago Generate** to build the multiworld — then host as normal!

---

## Feedback

Drop feedback in the Poképelago channel in the future-game-design channel on the [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago).

- **Game logic / apworld:** [dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago](https://github.com/dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago)
- **Web client:** [dowlle/PokepelagoClient](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient)

# Release v0.2.1

> ⚠️ **This is a beta release.** Expect bugs. Drop feedback in the Poképelago channel in [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago).

Welcome to [Poképelago](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/), a Pokémon collection and guessing game built for Archipelago Multiworld play!

## What's New

### v0.2.1 (this release)
- **Goal system:** Set a goal in your YAML: either a percentage of Pokémon to guess (`goal_type: percentage`) or a fixed count (`goal_type: count`). Reaching your goal now correctly signals completion to the server and triggers the Archipelago release for remaining items.
- **Multi-game generation fix:** Generations with Poképelago alongside other games no longer fail depending on alphabetical player order. (The item pool sizing was incorrectly using the global multiworld pool instead of the player-scoped count.)
- **Client tracker fix:** The Checked counter now only counts guessed Pokémon, milestone and Oak's Lab locations were previously being counted too. The total now correctly reflects the Pokémon count for your selected generation instead of always showing 1025.

### v0.2.0
- **Sprites without downloading:** Just paste a sprite repo URL (like the [PokeAPI sprites](https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites/tree/master/sprites)) into Settings and sprites load automatically. No ZIP, no upload required.
- **Better connection handling:** The game now reliably detects when the server goes offline and shows you the connection quality in real time.
- **Name guessing improvements:** Pokémon with accents, dots, or localized names (Japanese, French, etc.) are now recognized correctly.
- **Stability:** Various generation and logic fixes that prevented some seeds from generating. For stability reasons, only generation 1 through 3 are accessible for now.

---

## How to Play

### For Players

1. **Setup Your YAML:** Download the [pokepelago.yaml](cci:7://file:///f:/pythonProjects/ArchipelagoPokepelago/Players/pokepelago.yaml:0:0-0:0) from this release. Edit it to pick your generation and goal, then hand it to your host.
2. **Open the game:** Go to **[https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/)** — please be aware, this is a new URL! The old URL will remain available to support old generations (for now).
3. **Load sprites (optional but recommended):** In Settings → *Sprite Repo URL*, paste this link and sprites will load live:
   `https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites/tree/master/sprites`
   Or upload your own sprite folder the old way. No sprites? The game still plays fully.
4. **Connect:** Hit the gear icon, enter your server address, slot name, and password, then click **Connect**. Start guessing Pokémon names, a correct guess means you caught it!

### For Hosts

1. Download the `pokepelago.apworld` from this release.
2. Place it in your Archipelago installation's `custom_worlds` folder.
3. Collect [pokepelago.yaml](cci:7://file:///f:/pythonProjects/ArchipelagoPokepelago/Players/pokepelago.yaml:0:0-0:0) files from your players and put them in your `Players` folder.
4. Run **Archipelago Generate** to build the multiworld, then host as normal!

---

## Feedback

Drop feedback in the Poképelago channel in the future-game-design channel on the [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago).

- **Game logic / apworld:** [dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago](https://github.com/dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago)
- **Web client:** [dowlle/PokepelagoClient](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient)

# Release v0.3.1

> ⚠️ **This is a beta release.** Expect bugs. Drop feedback in the Poképelago channel in [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago).

Welcome to [Poképelago](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/), a Pokémon collection and guessing game built for Archipelago Multiworld play!

## What's New

### v0.3.1 (this release)
- **New APWorld logic:** Another full rewrite of the APWorld logic has drastically improved generation time. However, dexsanity unlocks have been removed, as they basically made generation mathematically impossible. Dexsanity enabled in the YAML will now add a location to every guessable Pokémon only (no need to unlock them anymore). The new setup is much cleaner and can generate multiple Poképelago games within a second. 
- **Added gen 4-9:** Gen 4 to 9 have been re-added to the game. 
- **Added region locks:** Region locks have been re-added to the game.
- **Derpemon sprites and traps:** Our amazing community has created 100+ sprites already for the Derpemon project! These can be toggled on and off for all sprites or can be triggered by the new **derpemon trap**. Check out [the project](https://github.com/TheShadowOfLight/DerpemonCommunityProject/tree/main) if you want to contribute.
- **Shuffle and release trap:** The dexgrid can now be shuffled by the shuffle trap and the release trap will release a Pokémon that you have to re-catch. You can turn off the trap chance in the YAML.
- **Game manager:** Running multiple Poképelago games? With the game manager, you can save your connection details and quickly switch between games.
- **Activity history filter:** Filter by your own items and locations in the activity history to see only what you sent and received.
- **Location items send:** In the Pokémon details screen, you can see what item that Pokémon sent to the multiworld. 

---

## How to Play

### For Players

1. **Setup Your YAML:** Download the pokepelago.yaml from this release. Edit it to pick your generations and goal, then hand it to your host.
2. **Open the game:** Go to **[https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/)**.
3. **Load sprites (optional but recommended):** In Settings → *Sprite Repo URL*, paste this link and sprites will load live:
   `https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites/tree/master/sprites`
   Or upload your own sprite folder the old way. No sprites? The game still plays fully.
4. **Connect:** Hit the gear icon, enter your server address, slot name, and password, then click **Connect**. Start guessing Pokémon names, a correct guess means you caught it!

### For Hosts

1. Download the `pokepelago.apworld` from this release.
2. Place it in your Archipelago installation's `custom_worlds` folder.
3. Collect pokepelago.yaml files from your players and put them in your `Players` folder.
4. Run **Archipelago Generate** to build the multiworld, then host as normal!

---

## Feedback

Drop feedback in the Poképelago channel in the future-game-design channel on the [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago).

- **Game logic / apworld repo:** [dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago](https://github.com/dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago)
- **Web client repo:** [dowlle/PokepelagoClient](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient)

# Release v0.3.3

Quick patch for options template

> ⚠️ **This is a beta release.** Expect bugs. Drop feedback in the Poképelago channel in [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago).

Welcome to [Poképelago](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/), a Pokémon collection and guessing game built for Archipelago Multiworld play!

## What's New

### v0.3.2 (this release)
- **Milestone logic fix:** Type milestones (e.g. "Caught 20 Water Pokémon") and global milestones (e.g. "Guessed 250 Pokémon") now have proper access rules. Previously, milestones were considered immediately reachable by Archipelago's logic even if the required Pokémon were locked behind Region Passes or Type Keys. Milestone locations are now only considered in logic once enough Pokémon are actually accessible based on your current items. This prevents incorrect item placement in multiworld seeds.
- **Trap weights:** The YAML now supports a trap_weights option to control how often each trap type appears. Set any trap type to 0 to disable it entirely. The overall trap rate is still controlled by trap_chance.
- **Language selector:** A language selector has been added next to the Pokémon name input. Select a specific language to only accept guesses in that language, preventing accidental catches from shorter names in other languages (e.g. German "Dodu" auto-completing Doduo). Defaults to Global (all languages accepted). Your selection is saved between sessions.
- **Type proficiency counter:** The type proficiency overview now shows how many Pokémon of each type you have guessed out of the total available, e.g. Fire (12/15).
- **Type milestone fix:** Type-specific milestones were not being sent to the multiworld for players on the new APWorld version. This has been fixed, and any missed milestones will be retroactively sent on reconnect.

### v0.3.1 (previous release)
- **New APWorld logic:** Another full rewrite of the APWorld logic has drastically improved generation time. However, dexsanity unlocks have been removed, as they basically made generation mathematically impossible. Dexsanity enabled in the YAML will now add a location to every guessable Pokémon only (no need to unlock them anymore). The new setup is much cleaner and can generate multiple Poképelago games within a second. 
- **Added gen 4-9:** Gen 4 to 9 have been re-added to the game. 
- **Added region locks:** Region locks have been re-added to the game.
- **Derpemon sprites and traps:** Our amazing community has created 100+ sprites already for the Derpemon project! These can be toggled on and off for all sprites or can be triggered by the new **derpemon trap**. Check out [the project](https://github.com/TheShadowOfLight/DerpemonCommunityProject/tree/main) if you want to contribute.
- **Shuffle and release trap:** The dexgrid can now be shuffled by the shuffle trap and the release trap will release a Pokémon that you have to re-catch. You can turn off the trap chance in the YAML.
- **Game manager:** Running multiple Poképelago games? With the game manager, you can save your connection details and quickly switch between games.
- **Activity history filter:** Filter by your own items and locations in the activity history to see only what you sent and received.
- **Location items send:** In the Pokémon details screen, you can see what item that Pokémon sent to the multiworld. 

---

## How to Play

### For Players

1. **Setup Your YAML:** Download the pokepelago.yaml from this release. Edit it to pick your generations and goal, then hand it to your host.
2. **Open the game:** Go to **[https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/)**.
3. **Load sprites (optional but recommended):** In Settings → *Sprite Repo URL*, paste this link and sprites will load live:
   `https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites/tree/master/sprites`
   Or upload your own sprite folder the old way. No sprites? The game still plays fully.
4. **Connect:** Hit the gear icon, enter your server address, slot name, and password, then click **Connect**. Start guessing Pokémon names, a correct guess means you caught it!

### For Hosts

1. Download the `pokepelago.apworld` from this release.
2. Place it in your Archipelago installation's `custom_worlds` folder.
3. Collect pokepelago.yaml files from your players and put them in your `Players` folder.
4. Run **Archipelago Generate** to build the multiworld, then host as normal!

---

## Feedback

Drop feedback in the Poképelago channel in the future-game-design channel on the [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago).

- **Game logic / apworld repo:** [dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago](https://github.com/dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago)
- **Web client repo:** [dowlle/PokepelagoClient](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient)

v0.4.0.description:
Release v0.4.0

This is a beta release. Expect bugs. Drop feedback in the Pokepelago channel in Archipelago Discord.

Welcome to Pokepelago, a Pokemon collection and guessing game built for Archipelago Multiworld play!

This APWorld release was originally built for Archipelago 0.6.7, but I backported it to 0.6.6. It was tested, but be advised - bugs may happen.

What's New

APWorld v0.4.0

  • Gate lock system: A whole new layer of progression. Gate specific Pokemon categories behind unique items that enter the multiworld pool:
    • Legendary Locks (Gym Badges): Collect 6 Badges for sub-legendaries (trios, regis, tapus, etc.), 7 for box legendaries (version mascots), and 8 for mythics (Mew, Celebi, Arceus, etc.). 8 Gym Badge items are added to the pool.
    • Trade Evolution Lock (Link Cable): Require a Link Cable before guessing trade-evolved Pokemon like Alakazam, Machamp, Golem, Gengar, Scizor, and more.
    • Baby Pokemon Lock (Daycare): Require Daycare item(s) before guessing baby Pokemon (Pichu, Cleffa, Togepi, Tyrogue, etc.). Configurable how many Daycare items are needed (1-5).
    • Fossil Lock (Fossil Restorer): Require a Fossil Restorer before guessing fossil Pokemon (Omanyte, Kabuto, Aerodactyl, Cranidos, etc.).
    • Ultra Beast Lock (Ultra Wormhole): Require an Ultra Wormhole before guessing Ultra Beasts (Nihilego through Blacephalon, plus Necrozma).
    • Paradox Lock (Time Rift): Require a Time Rift before guessing Paradox Pokemon (Great Tusk, Roaring Moon, Iron Valiant, Koraidon, Miraidon, etc.).
    • Stone Evolution Lock: Require the matching evolutionary stone (Fire, Water, Thunder, Leaf, Moon, Sun, Shiny, Dusk, Dawn, Ice) before guessing stone-only evolutions. Each stone type that gates at least one active Pokemon adds one stone item to the pool.
    • All locks are independently toggleable in your YAML. Mix and match for your desired complexity!
  • Starter region selection: Choose which game region your adventure starts in via the starter_region YAML option. Pick a specific region (Kanto, Johto, Hoenn, etc.) or set to any for a random active region each seed.
  • Starter Pokemon selection: Choose which starter Pokemon you begin with via starter_pokemon. Pick by position (first/second/third) or any for random. Kanto: Bulbasaur/Charmander/Squirtle, Johto: Chikorita/Cyndaquil/Totodile, etc.
  • Starting location count: Configure how many free "Oak's Lab" starting locations are included (0-8) via starting_location_count. These are immediately accessible checks that kickstart your adventure. Set to 0 for a harder start.
  • Accurate goals: Percentage and count goals no longer snap to milestones. Set any target count up to the total number of guessable Pokemon. The goal is calculated precisely based on your active regions and starter Pokemon.
  • Filler item weights: Control the relative weight of each filler item category (Master Ball, Pokeballs, Medicine, Key Items, Splash) via filler_weights. Set any category to 0 to disable it entirely.
  • Shiny tokens: A new cosmetic filler item type. Receiving a Shiny Token makes a random caught Pokemon display its shiny sprite. Toggle on/off with include_shinies.
  • Integration tests: Full test suite covering all lock combinations, region combos, and edge cases. 59 tests pass across all configurations.

Client v0.4.0

The web client has also been updated with features to support the new APWorld:

  • Item Tracker sidebar: New sidebar panel showing all your gate items (Gym Badges, Link Cable, Daycare, Fossil Restorer, Ultra Wormhole, Time Rift, Evo Stones, Region Passes) with real-time progress.
  • Interactive lock badges: Missing requirement badges are clickable. Tap once to highlight, tap again to request an AP hint for that item.
  • Category filtering: Click any obtained item in the Item Tracker to filter the dex grid by that category (legendary tier, trade evos, fossils, Ultra Beasts, Paradox, stone evolutions, region).
  • Localized names everywhere: Pokemon names appear in your selected language across the entire UI.
  • Diacritics support: Accented names like "Flabebe" match correctly regardless of how you type them.
  • Co-op sync: Multiple players on the same AP slot see each other's guesses in real time.
  • World hop stability: Disconnecting fully resets all game state. No more stale sprites or phantom filters.
  • Dex number overlay: Grid tiles show National Dex numbers. Larger and centered on empty tiles, small corner overlay with sprites.
  • HTTPS connection fix: Automatic secure WebSocket detection with clearer error messages.

YAML Options Reference

Option | Type | Default | Description -- | -- | -- | -- dexsanity | Toggle | On | Per-Pokemon location checks type_locks | Toggle | On | Require Type Keys to guess Pokemon region_locks | Toggle | On | Require Region Passes for non-starting regions starter_region | Choice | any | Starting region selection starter_pokemon | Choice | any | Starting Pokemon selection starting_location_count | 0-8 | 8 | Number of free starting checks legendary_locks | Toggle | Off | Gate legendaries behind Gym Badges trade_locks | Toggle | Off | Require Link Cable for trade evolutions baby_locks | Toggle | Off | Require Daycare for baby Pokemon daycare_count | 1-5 | 1 | Daycare items needed fossil_locks | Toggle | Off | Require Fossil Restorer for fossils ultra_beast_locks | Toggle | Off | Require Ultra Wormhole for Ultra Beasts paradox_locks | Toggle | Off | Require Time Rift for Paradox Pokemon stone_locks | Toggle | Off | Require matching stone for stone evolutions include_shinies | Toggle | Off | Add Shiny Token filler items filler_weights | Weights | varied | Control filler item distribution trap_weights | Weights | varied | Control trap type distribution

How to Play

For Players

  1. Setup Your YAML: Download the pokepelago.yaml from this release. Edit it to pick your generations, locks, and goal, then hand it to your host.
  2. Open the game: Go to https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/.
  3. Load sprites (optional but recommended): In Settings → Sprite Repo URL, paste this link and sprites will load live:
    https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites/tree/master/sprites
    Or upload your own sprite folder the old way. No sprites? The game still plays fully.
  4. Connect: Hit the gear icon, enter your server address, slot name, and password, then click Connect. Start guessing Pokemon names, a correct guess means you caught it!

For Hosts

  1. Download the pokepelago.apworld from this release.
  2. Place it in your Archipelago installation's custom_worlds folder.
  3. Collect pokepelago.yaml files from your players and put them in your Players folder.
  4. Run Archipelago Generate to build the multiworld, then host as normal!

Feedback

Drop feedback in the Pokepelago channel in the future-game-design channel on the Archipelago Discord.

  • Game logic / apworld repo: dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago
  • Web client repo: dowlle/PokepelagoClient

# Release v0.4.3

> **This is a beta release.** Expect bugs. Drop feedback in the Pokepelago channel in [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago).

Welcome to [Pokepelago](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/), a Pokemon collection and guessing game built for Archipelago Multiworld play!

> **This APWorld is built and tested for Archipelago 0.6.6.**

## What's New in v0.4.3

### APWorld v0.4.3

- **Stone evolution data fix:** Added 8 missing Pokemon to stone evolution groups, verified against PokeAPI using latest-gen evolution methods:
  - **Ice Stone:** Crabominable, Cetitan (previously only Glaceon)
  - **Thunder Stone:** Magnezone, Probopass, Vikavolt, Bellibolt (previously only Raichu, Jolteon, Eelektross)
  - **Leaf Stone:** Leafeon (changed from Moss Rock to Leaf Stone in Gen 8+)
  - **Fire Stone:** Scovillain (Gen 9 Fire Stone evolution)
- **Legacy YAML backward compatibility:** Old YAMLs using `include_kanto`/`include_johto`/etc. toggles now correctly merge into the unified `regions` option. Players upgrading from pre-v0.4.0 no longer need to rewrite their YAMLs.
- **Options visibility fix:** Fixed `TypeError` in `get_option_groups` caused by legacy region toggles using a plain `int` for visibility instead of the `Visibility` enum. This broke the fuzzer and could affect option display in the web UI.

### What was new in v0.4.2

- **Hisui-only fix:** Fixed FillError when Hisui is the only active region by forcing at least 5 starting locations. Hisui's 7 dual-type Pokemon left too few ungated slots for progression items.
- **Dexsanity-off milestone fix:** Added extra early milestone steps (3, 4) when dexsanity is off, giving small regions enough locations for progression item placement.
- **Filler item cleanup:** Removed unused filler categories (pokeballs, medicine, repels). Filler pool is now: Master Ball, Pokedex, Pokegear, and Splash.
- **AP 0.6.6 compatibility:** Verified and tested against stock Archipelago 0.6.6. Minimum AP version lowered from 0.6.7 to 0.6.6.
- **CI/CD cleanup:** Removed upstream Archipelago workflows. Only pokepelago-specific tests, fuzz, and release workflows remain.

### What was new in v0.4.0

- **Gate lock system:** A whole new layer of progression. Gate specific Pokemon categories behind unique items that enter the multiworld pool:
  - **Legendary Locks (Gym Badges):** Collect 6 Badges for sub-legendaries (trios, regis, tapus, etc.), 7 for box legendaries (version mascots), and 8 for mythics (Mew, Celebi, Arceus, etc.). 8 Gym Badge items are added to the pool.
  - **Trade Evolution Lock (Link Cable):** Require a Link Cable before guessing trade-evolved Pokemon like Alakazam, Machamp, Golem, Gengar, Scizor, and more.
  - **Baby Pokemon Lock (Daycare):** Require Daycare item(s) before guessing baby Pokemon (Pichu, Cleffa, Togepi, Tyrogue, etc.). Configurable how many Daycare items are needed (1-5).
  - **Fossil Lock (Fossil Restorer):** Require a Fossil Restorer before guessing fossil Pokemon (Omanyte, Kabuto, Aerodactyl, Cranidos, etc.).
  - **Ultra Beast Lock (Ultra Wormhole):** Require an Ultra Wormhole before guessing Ultra Beasts (Nihilego through Blacephalon, plus Necrozma).
  - **Paradox Lock (Time Rift):** Require a Time Rift before guessing Paradox Pokemon (Great Tusk, Roaring Moon, Iron Valiant, Koraidon, Miraidon, etc.).
  - **Stone Evolution Lock:** Require the matching evolutionary stone (Fire, Water, Thunder, Leaf, Moon, Sun, Shiny, Dusk, Dawn, Ice) before guessing stone-only evolutions. Each stone type that gates at least one active Pokemon adds one stone item to the pool.
  - All locks are independently toggleable in your YAML. Mix and match for your desired complexity!
- **Starter region selection:** Choose which game region your adventure starts in via the `starter_region` YAML option. Pick a specific region (Kanto, Johto, Hoenn, etc.) or set to `any` for a random active region each seed.
- **Starter Pokemon selection:** Choose which starter Pokemon you begin with via `starter_pokemon`. Pick by position (first/second/third) or `any` for random. Kanto: Bulbasaur/Charmander/Squirtle, Johto: Chikorita/Cyndaquil/Totodile, etc.
- **Starting location count:** Configure how many free "Oak's Lab" starting locations are included (0-8) via `starting_location_count`. These are immediately accessible checks that kickstart your adventure. Set to 0 for a harder start.
- **Accurate goals:** Percentage and count goals no longer snap to milestones. Set any target count up to the total number of guessable Pokemon. The goal is calculated precisely based on your active regions and starter Pokemon.
- **Filler item weights:** Control the relative weight of each filler item category (Master Ball, Key Items, Splash) via `filler_weights`. Set any category to 0 to disable it entirely.
- **Shiny tokens:** A new cosmetic filler item type. Receiving a Shiny Token makes a random caught Pokemon display its shiny sprite. Toggle on/off with `include_shinies`.
- **Integration tests:** Full test suite covering all lock combinations, region combos, and edge cases. 59 tests pass across all configurations.

### Client v0.4.0

The web client has also been updated with features to support the new APWorld:

- **Item Tracker sidebar:** New sidebar panel showing all your gate items (Gym Badges, Link Cable, Daycare, Fossil Restorer, Ultra Wormhole, Time Rift, Evo Stones, Region Passes) with real-time progress.
- **Interactive lock badges:** Missing requirement badges are clickable. Tap once to highlight, tap again to request an AP hint for that item.
- **Category filtering:** Click any obtained item in the Item Tracker to filter the dex grid by that category (legendary tier, trade evos, fossils, Ultra Beasts, Paradox, stone evolutions, region).
- **Localized names everywhere:** Pokemon names appear in your selected language across the entire UI.
- **Diacritics support:** Accented names like "Flabebe" match correctly regardless of how you type them.
- **Co-op sync:** Multiple players on the same AP slot see each other's guesses in real time.
- **World hop stability:** Disconnecting fully resets all game state. No more stale sprites or phantom filters.
- **Dex number overlay:** Grid tiles show National Dex numbers. Larger and centered on empty tiles, small corner overlay with sprites.
- **HTTPS connection fix:** Automatic secure WebSocket detection with clearer error messages.

### YAML Options Reference

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `dexsanity` | Toggle | On | Per-Pokemon location checks |
| `type_locks` | Toggle | On | Require Type Keys to guess Pokemon |
| `region_locks` | Toggle | On | Require Region Passes for non-starting regions |
| `starter_region` | Choice | any | Starting region selection |
| `starter_pokemon` | Choice | any | Starting Pokemon selection |
| `starting_location_count` | 0-8 | 8 | Number of free starting checks |
| `legendary_locks` | Toggle | Off | Gate legendaries behind Gym Badges |
| `trade_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Link Cable for trade evolutions |
| `baby_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Daycare for baby Pokemon |
| `daycare_count` | 1-5 | 1 | Daycare items needed |
| `fossil_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Fossil Restorer for fossils |
| `ultra_beast_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Ultra Wormhole for Ultra Beasts |
| `paradox_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Time Rift for Paradox Pokemon |
| `stone_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require matching stone for stone evolutions |
| `include_shinies` | Toggle | On | Add Shiny Token filler items |
| `filler_weights` | Weights | varied | Control filler item distribution |
| `trap_weights` | Weights | varied | Control trap type distribution |

---

## How to Play

### For Players

1. **Setup Your YAML:** Download the pokepelago.yaml from this release. Edit it to pick your generations, locks, and goal, then hand it to your host.
2. **Open the game:** Go to **[https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/)**.
3. **Load sprites (optional but recommended):** In Settings → *Sprite Repo URL*, paste this link and sprites will load live:
   `https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites/tree/master/sprites`
   Or upload your own sprite folder the old way. No sprites? The game still plays fully.
4. **Connect:** Hit the gear icon, enter your server address, slot name, and password, then click **Connect**. Start guessing Pokemon names, a correct guess means you caught it!

### For Hosts

1. Download the `pokepelago.apworld` from this release.
2. Place it in your Archipelago installation's `custom_worlds` folder.
3. Collect pokepelago.yaml files from your players and put them in your `Players` folder.
4. Run **Archipelago Generate** to build the multiworld, then host as normal!

---

## Feedback

Drop feedback in the Pokepelago channel in the future-game-design channel on the [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago).

- **Game logic / apworld repo:** [dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago](https://github.com/dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago)
- **Web client repo:** [dowlle/PokepelagoClient](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient)

| `legendary_locks` | Toggle | Off | Gate legendaries behind Gym Badges |
| `trade_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Link Cable for trade evolutions |
| `baby_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Daycare for baby Pokemon |
| `daycare_count` | 1-5 | 1 | Daycare items needed |
| `fossil_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Fossil Restorer for fossils |
| `ultra_beast_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Ultra Wormhole for Ultra Beasts |
| `paradox_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Time Rift for Paradox Pokemon |
| `stone_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require matching stone for stone evolutions |
| `include_shinies` | Toggle | On | Add Shiny Token filler items |
| `filler_weights` | Weights | varied | Control filler item distribution |
| `trap_weights` | Weights | varied | Control trap type distribution |

---

## How to Play

### For Players

1. **Setup Your YAML:** Download the pokepelago.yaml from this release. Edit it to pick your generations, locks, and goal, then hand it to your host.
2. **Open the game:** Go to **[https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/)**.
3. **Load sprites (optional but recommended):** In Settings, find *Sprite Repo URL* and paste this link so sprites load live:
   `https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites/tree/master/sprites`
   Or upload your own sprite folder the old way. No sprites? The game still plays fully.
4. **Connect:** Hit the gear icon, enter your server address, slot name, and password, then click **Connect**. Start guessing Pokemon names, a correct guess means you caught it!

### For Hosts

1. Download the `pokepelago.apworld` from this release.
2. Place it in your Archipelago installation's `custom_worlds` folder.
3. Collect pokepelago.yaml files from your players and put them in your `Players` folder.
4. Run **Archipelago Generate** to build the multiworld, then host as normal!

---

## Feedback

Drop feedback in the Pokepelago channel in the future-game-design channel on the [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago).

- **Game logic / apworld repo:** [dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago](https://github.com/dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago)

v0.6.1.description:
# Release v0.6.1

> **This is a beta release.** The APWorld passed the full 14,000-run fuzzer suite and the client stack was smoke-tested against a multi-slot multiworld before tagging, but some seed configurations may still hit completion issues in the client. If a game stops letting you make progress toward the goal, please report it in the [Pokepelago Discord channel](https://discord.com/channels/731205301247803413/1474030797811093514) and include your YAML so we can reproduce. Not in the Archipelago Discord yet? [Join here](https://discord.gg/archipelago) first, then open the channel above.

Welcome to [Pokepelago](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/), a Pokemon collection and guessing game built for Archipelago Multiworld play!

> ⚠ **Breaking change in v0.6.1:** this release requires **Archipelago 0.6.7 or later**. Download it here: [Archipelago 0.6.7 release](https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases/tag/0.6.7). The APWorld now uses the `rule_builder` module introduced in that version. If you (or your host) are still on AP 0.6.6, upgrade Archipelago before running a seed with this apworld, otherwise generation will fail with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'rule_builder'`.

> **This APWorld is built and tested for Archipelago 0.6.7.**

## What's New in v0.6.1

This is the biggest release since v0.4.0. Progression v2 lands a full new layer of gating by routes, evolution lines, and badge level. The web client gets a performance overhaul that makes a 1000-slot dex grid stay responsive even during heavy play, a redesigned settings modal, a themeable silhouette treatment for unguessed Pokemon, and a version indicator that tells players when the APWorld the host is running has fallen behind the latest release.

### APWorld v0.6.1

#### Progression v2

- **Route Locks.** Gate Pokemon by game area instead of by individual route. The 306 individual routes from previous versions are now grouped into 55 meaningful areas (for example Melemele Island, Wild Area North, Hoenn Early Routes) plus 25 virtual and roaming routes, for a total of 80 Route Keys. Each Route Key covers an area; a Pokemon is accessible if you have ANY area key for a route it appears on. Forces Dexsanity ON because the item pool needs the extra locations.
- **Line Locks.** Gate Pokemon by evolution line. One Line Unlock item per family unlocks the whole line. Auto disables when Route Locks is also on, since the two systems conflict on fill ratios.
- **Badge Level Gating.** Tie gym badges to level caps across the dex. Pokemon with higher base stats need more badges regardless of which legendary tier they are in.

#### Other APWorld improvements

- **Starter fallback for edge region configs.** New 3 tier fallback in `_select_random_starters` handles the Hisui only plus `random_region_count: 1` edge case. Previously a seed that rolled Hisui as the single active region with `lab_only` starters could fail with `FillError: No more spots to place 11 items` because Hisui has 0 lab starters in data. Zero effect on any seed where the primary candidate pool is non empty.
- **FEAT-11: APWorld version in slot_data.** The APWorld now publishes its version string in `slot_data.apworld_version`. The web client reads this to show players which version the host is running. Legacy APWorlds without this field display nothing, so no false outdated warnings.
- **BUG-08 fix: manual regions list now honored.** `random_region_count` default changed from 3 to 0. Previously, a YAML with an explicit `regions:` list would silently ignore it because the default triggered random region selection. Now the manual list is respected when `random_region_count` is unset.
- **Determinism fix.** Sets were being iterated in hash dependent order during `create_items()` and slot data serialization, causing "same items, different order" failures across different `PYTHONHASHSEED` values. Fixed by sorting all set iterations.
- **FillError prevention for small regions.** New `_prune_invalid_gates()` pass disables category gates when zero matching Pokemon exist in the active regions, disables badge gating when the guessable pool is under 20, and forces a minimum number of starting locations by gate count. Prevents crashes on Hisui only seeds and similarly small configs.
- **Rule builder migration.** Minimum Archipelago version bumped to 0.6.7. Internal logic now uses the `rule_builder` module (`Has`, `HasAll`, `HasAllCounts`, `HasAny`) for cleaner gate rule composition and to unblock future entrance randomization work. This is the source of the breaking-change callout at the top of these notes.
- **Docs clarification.** The `route_locks` YAML key is technically `route_locks_enabled` (this is the one option where the dataclass attribute name differs from the class name). Now documented directly in the `RouteLocks` option docstring so the correct key is discoverable without hunting through the Options dataclass.

### Client v0.6.1

#### Gate item tracker and filters

- **Item Tracker sidebar.** New sidebar section that shows your progress on every gate item class in use for your current seed: Gym Badges (legendary tier), Link Cable (trade evolutions), Daycare (baby Pokemon), Fossil Restorer, Ultra Wormhole, Time Rift, Evo Stones, Region Passes, Route Keys, and Line Unlocks. Each row shows a running count or fill bar so you can see how close the next tier is without checking the log.
- **Interactive lock badges.** Missing requirement badges are clickable. Tap once to highlight and tap again to send an AP `!hint` for that item.
- **Category filtering.** Click any obtained item in the Item Tracker to filter the dex grid by that category (for example legendary tier, trade evos, fossils, Ultra Beasts, Paradox, stone evolutions, regions, routes).
- **Route clearing display.** Per region route progress with expandable per route catch detail. Shows which routes are unlocked, which are cleared (threshold reached), and which still need catches.

#### Sprites and visuals

- **Who's That Pokemon silhouette.** Unguessed and hinted slots now render as a silhouette with a subtle indigo halo (warm red in the Pokemon theme). Themeable via the `--pp-silhouette-glow` CSS variable. Toggleable in Settings if you prefer pure flat silhouettes.
- **Sprite size option.** Five discrete sizes in the Sprites tab: 1x, 1.25x, 1.5x, 1.75x, and 2x. Sprites and overlays scale together. Default stays at 1x so returning players see no change.
- **Pokedex and Pokegear now force silhouette.** Using a Pokedex or Pokegear on a locked Pokemon now reveals its silhouette even when the Enable Shadows setting is off. Previously the reveal action was invisible in that config.
- **Derpyfication fix.** After a Derp Trap hits, caught Pokemon used to blank out across the whole grid. Now only the specific slot that got derpyfied refreshes. Derpemon sprites also show correctly in the details modal, not just in the grid.

#### PokemonDetails modal upgrade

- **Shadow silhouette in modal.** Unguessed Pokemon show as a silhouette inside the details modal when Enable Shadows is on, matching the grid behavior.
- **Keyboard navigation.** `A` and `D` cycle through the next and previous unguessed Pokemon (scope aware, skips released and checked). Left and right arrow keys cycle through any Pokemon in the full dex. Wraps around at the ends. Side chevron buttons on the modal mirror the arrow keys.
- **Global guess input stays usable.** You can now type a guess in the top bar input while the details modal is open. Previously the modal intercepted clicks.
- **Always Show Types setting.** New toggle that reveals type badges in PokemonDetails before the Pokemon is guessed. Handy for type fans who want the extra context while playing.

#### Settings modal redesign

- **Tabbed modal.** The full settings panel now lives in a modal with Interface, Sprites, and Twitch tabs. The sidebar keeps quick filters (Connection, Generations, Sprite Set) with a "Preferences and Appearance" link to the modal.
- **Grouped toggles.** Interface settings are grouped under Grid Layout, Type Display, Catch Feedback, and Integrations, each with a matching preview icon next to the subheading.
- **Pokemon theme.** A second visual theme alongside the default dark one. Red and gold palette, warm region accents, and region card backgrounds. Switch via the Theme cards at the top of the Interface tab.
- **Escape to close.** All dismissible modals (Settings, Connection Manager, Credits, Pokemon Details) close on the Escape key.

#### Version indicator

- **Server version row in the sidebar.** When you are connected in Archipelago mode the sidebar now shows `Server: v0.6.1` for hosts running the matching APWorld version. If the host is running an older version, an amber "update to vX.Y.Z" link appears pointing at the latest release. You cannot switch APWorlds mid seed, but you can ask the host to update for the next one.
- **Caches the latest release info for 24 hours.** One GitHub API check per day. Silent fallback if the API is unreachable.

#### Performance

- **Narrow context for slot renders.** Catching one Pokemon no longer re-renders the other 1024 slots. React.memo on PokemonSlot is now effective because the slot subscribes only to a tight context of UI settings and sprite URLs, and per Pokemon state flows in as props.
- **Batched item receive.** When the server sends a burst of items at connect time or during catch up, each field updates in a single pass instead of rerendering on every item. A 200 item batch that used to trigger dozens of renders per field now does one.
- **Collapsed regions unmount.** Region cards that you collapse are removed from the DOM instead of hidden with CSS, cutting hundreds of slots from the render tree in typical play configs. Sprites re-fetch from the local cache on expand, usually in under a tenth of a second.
- **Memoized guess evaluation.** `isPokemonGuessable` results are cached per Pokemon ID until their inputs change. Previously ran the full gate evaluation once per render for every slot.
- **CSS transitions tuned.** Replaced blanket `transition-all` with specific transitions on the hot slot path, reducing layout thrash on slower devices.

#### Bug fixes

- **Game state isolation across AP games.** Switching between Archipelago servers or slots without a clean disconnect used to leak state. Pokegear counts, type unlocks, region passes, route keys, gym badges, and active region config could carry over into the next game. Now a full reset fires on every connect, and the new game seeds fresh from the server's slot data and DataStorage.
- **Derpemon sprite reset on game switch.** Related to the above. Derpyfied Pokemon from a previous game no longer persist into the new game's grid.
- **Silhouette alt attribute spoiler.** Unguessed Pokemon briefly displayed their lowercase name as browser fallback text while the image loaded. Fixed by revealing the name only after the Pokemon is guessed. Unguessed slots show `Pokemon #123` instead.
- **Guided tour z index.** The tour now sits above every modal unconditionally, including the redesigned Settings modal. Also auto closes the Settings modal for steps that do not need it (steps 1 to 7), preventing the modal from obscuring its own target.
- **Release trap recaught behavior.** Recaught Pokemon no longer re-release on reconnect when the server has stored historical release data.
- **Reconnect state handling.** The reconnect flow properly resets state before syncing server DataStorage, fixing a brief flash of stale values. The dexsanity off warning modal is removed.

#### DevEx and infrastructure

- **Branch protection.** `main` and `beta` now require CI to pass before merge, block force pushes, and block deletions. Ensures every tagged release has green CI.
- **CI as a quality gate again.** Two pre existing lint errors that had been quietly breaking CI since early April are now silenced with targeted disables, and the workflow is green again. Regressions will actually show up.
- **Dependency hardening.** All 28 packages pinned to exact versions. Patched vite 7.3.1 to 7.3.2 (path traversal plus WebSocket file read), picomatch (method injection and ReDoS), and brace expansion (zero step hang). `npm audit` reports zero vulnerabilities.
- **CSP for canvas confetti and GitHub API.** Blob workers now allowed so the catch celebration confetti and the version check fetch work in stricter security contexts.
- **In game changelog updated.** The Credits modal's in game changelog now lists v0.5.0, v0.5.1, and v0.6.1 with the full set of bullets. Stale "Upcoming" items from old APWorlds have been stripped.
- **Branch cleanup.** Twelve stale feature branches from the v0.6 development cycle have been deleted from both local and origin. The client repo is back to a clean `main`, `beta`, and `gh-pages`.

## Previous releases

Full notes for earlier versions are on the release pages so this one can stay readable.

- [v0.5.1](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient/releases/tag/v0.5.1) — milestone trigger fix, beta branch merge
- [v0.5.0](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient/releases/tag/v0.5.0) — milestone trigger fix initial release
- [v0.4.4](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient/releases/tag/v0.4.4) — stone evolution data fixes, type milestone slot_data export, legacy YAML compatibility
- [v0.4.2](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient/releases/tag/v0.4.2) — Hisui-only FillError fix, dexsanity-off milestone support, AP 0.6.6 compatibility
- [v0.4.0](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient/releases/tag/v0.4.0) — gate lock system (Legendary / Trade / Baby / Fossil / Ultra Beast / Paradox / Stone), starter region and Pokemon selection, starting location count, Item Tracker sidebar

## YAML Options Reference

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `dexsanity` | Toggle | On | Per Pokemon location checks |
| `type_locks` | Toggle | On | Require Type Keys to guess Pokemon |
| `region_locks` | Toggle | On | Require Region Passes for non starting regions |
| `route_locks_enabled` | Toggle | Off | Require Route Keys to access Pokemon by game area |
| `line_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Line Unlocks for evolution families |
| `badge_level_gating` | Toggle | Off | Tie gym badges to level caps across the dex |
| `starter_region` | Choice | any | Starting region selection |
| `starter_pokemon` | Choice | any | Starting Pokemon selection |
| `starting_location_count` | 0 to 8 | 8 | Number of free starting checks |
| `legendary_locks` | Toggle | Off | Gate legendaries behind Gym Badges |
| `trade_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Link Cable for trade evolutions |
| `baby_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Daycare for baby Pokemon |
| `daycare_count` | 1 to 5 | 1 | Daycare items needed |
| `fossil_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Fossil Restorer for fossils |
| `ultra_beast_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Ultra Wormhole for Ultra Beasts |
| `paradox_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require Time Rift for Paradox Pokemon |
| `stone_locks` | Toggle | Off | Require matching stone for stone evolutions |
| `random_region_count` | 0 to 10 | 0 | Random region selection (0 disables and uses the manual list) |
| `include_shinies` | Toggle | On | Add Shiny Token filler items |
| `trap_chance` | 0 to 100 | varied | Chance of a filler slot rolling a trap |

The full template with comments is attached to this release as `Pokepelago.yaml`.

---

## How to Play

### For Players

1. **Set up your YAML.** Download `Pokepelago.yaml` from this release. Edit it to pick your generations, locks, and goal, then hand it to your host.
2. **Open the game.** Go to [https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/](https://dowlle.github.io/PokepelagoClient/).
3. **Load sprites (optional but recommended).** In Preferences and Appearance, find the Sprite Repo URL and paste this link so sprites load live: `https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites/tree/master/sprites`. You can also upload your own sprite folder the old way. No sprites works fine too.
4. **Connect.** Open Settings, enter your server address, slot name, and password, then click Connect. Start guessing Pokemon names. A correct guess means you caught it.

### For Hosts

1. Download the `pokepelago.apworld` from this release.
2. Place it in your Archipelago installation's `custom_worlds` folder.
3. Collect Pokepelago YAML files from your players and put them in your `Players` folder.
4. Run Archipelago Generate to build the multiworld, then host as normal.

---

## Feedback

Drop feedback in the [Pokepelago channel](https://discord.com/channels/731205301247803413/1474030797811093514) on the [Archipelago Discord](https://discord.gg/archipelago). Bug reports with your YAML attached are the most useful thing we can get, especially anything that stops a game from being completable.

- **Game logic and APWorld repo:** [dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago](https://github.com/dowlle/ArchipelagoPokepelago)
- **Web client repo:** [dowlle/PokepelagoClient](https://github.com/dowlle/PokepelagoClient)
